  "text": "Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) is implemented across several layers:\n\n1. ROLE TYPE DEFINITION\n\npkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go is the foundational file. It defines RoleType as a string, the four constants (RoleNone, RoleViewer, RoleEditor, RoleAdmin), and the rolePrecedence map that establishes the hierarchy (None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40). The Includes(other RoleType) bool method uses this precedence map to check if a role encompasses a lower role. Parents() and Children() methods traverse the hierarchy.\n\npkg/services/org/model.go re-exports RoleType and the role constants from the identity package, and defines OrgUser (the DB model linking a user to an org with a RoleType role).\n\n2. ROLE-TO-PERMISSION MAPPING\n\npkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go defines BasicRolePrefix, FixedRolePrefix, RoleGrafanaAdmin, cloud role constants, BuildBasicRoleDefinitions() (builds basic role DTOs for basic:viewer/editor/admin/none/grafana_admin), and DeclareFixedRoles() (declares all OSS fixed roles and their grants to the three org roles).\n\npkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go and pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go declare fixed:folders:viewer/editor/admin and fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin roles with appropriate permission action sets, granted to corresponding basic org roles.\n\npkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/service.go implements the core access control service: DeclareFixedRoles, GetUserPermissions, getBasicRolePermissions, getCachedBasicRolesPermissions - resolving user permissions based on their basic role.\n\npkg/services/accesscontrol/database/database.go provides GetUsersBasicRoles (maps userID to role names) and GetBasicRolesPermissions (fetches all permissions assigned to basic roles via the builtin_role table).\n\n3. PERMISSION GUARD MIDDLEWARE\n\npkg/middleware/middleware.go defines: ReqGrafanaAdmin, ReqEditorRole = RoleAuth(org.RoleEditor, org.RoleAdmin), ReqOrgAdmin = RoleAuth(org.RoleAdmin).\n\npkg/middleware/auth.go implements RoleAuth(roles ...org.RoleType) web.Handler (checks if c.OrgRole matches any provided role, denies if not), Auth(options) web.Handler (checks sign-in/admin status), CanAdminPlugins, and RoleAppPluginAuth.\n\npkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go implements the RBAC-based middleware: Middleware(ac AccessControl) wraps an RBAC Evaluator into a web handler calling ac.Evaluate() against the signed-in user; authorize() injects URL params into scope templates; AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware resolves user identity in a target org before evaluating.\n\npkg/services/apiserver/auth/authorizer/role.go implements a Kubernetes API server authorizer: Admin gets full access, Editor gets get/list/watch/create/update/patch/delete/put/post, Viewer/None get only get/list/watch.\n\npkg/api/accesscontrol.go declares all HTTP server fixed roles via declareFixedRoles().\n\n4. FOLDER/DASHBOARD PERMISSION INHERITANCE\n\npkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go is the central inheritance file: defines all action/scope constants (ActionFoldersRead, ActionDashboardsWrite, ScopeFoldersPrefix, etc.); GetInheritedScopes() walks up the folder tree via folderSvc.GetParents() returning parent folder UID scopes; NewFolderUIDScopeResolver/NewFolderIDScopeResolver convert folder scopes to UID-based with parent scopes; NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver/NewDashboardIDScopeResolver resolve dashboard scopes including parent folder chain; resolveDashboardScope assembles all applicable scopes for a dashboard.\n\npkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go and dashboard_filter_no_subquery.go implement SQL-level permission filtering using recursive CTEs to traverse the folder hierarchy and apply inherited permissions.\n\npkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go implements IsInherited() to detect when a managed permission comes from a parent resource scope.\n\npkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go implements checkPermission and checkInheritedPermissions which traverse the folder tree for permission resolution.\n\npkg/services/authz/zanzana/translations.go maps basic role names to OpenFGA relation names (basic_viewer, basic_editor, basic_admin) for the Zanzana backend.\n\npkg/services/user/identity.go and pkg/services/authn/identity.go implement HasRole(role) using OrgRole.Includes(role) - enabling hierarchical checks where Admin passes a Viewer check."
}

  "text": "Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) is implemented across several layers:\n\n## 1. Role Type Definition\n\n**`pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go`** is the foundational file. It defines `RoleType` as a string, the four constants (`RoleNone`, `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, `RoleAdmin`), and the `rolePrecedence` map that establishes the hierarchy (None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40). The key `Includes(other RoleType) bool` method uses this precedence map to check if a role encompasses a lower role (e.g., Admin includes Editor and Viewer). `Parents()` and `Children()` methods also traverse the hierarchy.\n\n**`pkg/services/org/model.go`** re-exports `RoleType` and the role constants from the identity package, and defines `OrgUser` (the DB model linking a user to an org with a `RoleType` role).\n\n## 2. Role-to-Permission Mapping\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go`** defines:\n- Constants like `BasicRolePrefix`, `FixedRolePrefix`, `RoleGrafanaAdmin`, `FixedCloudViewerRole/EditorRole/AdminRole`\n- `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()`: builds the basic role DTOs (basic:viewer, basic:editor, basic:admin, basic:none, basic:grafana_admin) keyed by org role string\n- `DeclareFixedRoles(service, cfg)`: declares all OSS fixed roles and their grants to the three org roles\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`** and **`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`** declare the `fixed:folders:viewer/editor/admin` and `fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin` roles, mapping them to permission action sets and granting them to the corresponding basic org roles.\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/service.go`** implements the core access control service including `DeclareFixedRoles`, `GetUserPermissions`, `getBasicRolePermissions`, and `getCachedBasicRolesPermissions`—the logic that resolves what permissions a user has based on their basic role (Viewer/Editor/Admin).\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/database/database.go`** provides the store layer: `GetUsersBasicRoles` returns a map of userID→[]string of role names (Admin, Editor, Viewer, Grafana Admin), and `GetBasicRolesPermissions` fetches all permissions assigned to the user's basic roles via the `builtin_role` table.\n\n## 3. Permission Guard Middleware\n\n**`pkg/middleware/middleware.go`** defines the pre-built middleware guards:\n- `ReqGrafanaAdmin` — requires Grafana server admin\n- `ReqEditorRole = RoleAuth(org.RoleEditor, org.RoleAdmin)` — allows Editor or Admin org role\n- `ReqOrgAdmin = RoleAuth(org.RoleAdmin)` — allows only Admin org role\n\n**`pkg/middleware/auth.go`** implements:\n- `RoleAuth(roles ...org.RoleType) web.Handler`: checks if `c.OrgRole` exactly matches any of the provided roles; denies if not\n- `Auth(options *AuthOptions) web.Handler`: checks sign-in status and Grafana admin flag\n- `CanAdminPlugins`, `RoleAppPluginAuth`: additional guards using RBAC evaluators\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go`** implements the RBAC-based middleware:\n- `Middleware(ac AccessControl) func(Evaluator) web.Handler`: wraps an RBAC `Evaluator` (e.g., `EvalPermission(\"dashboards:read\", scope)`) into a web handler that calls `ac.Evaluate()` against the signed-in user\n- `authorize(c, ac, user, evaluator)`: injects URL params into scope templates and evaluates access\n- `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware`: resolves user identity in a target org before evaluating permissions\n\n**`pkg/services/apiserver/auth/authorizer/role.go`** implements the Kubernetes API server authorizer using org roles: Admin gets full access, Editor gets get/list/watch/create/update/patch/delete/put/post, and Viewer/None get only get/list/watch.\n\n**`pkg/api/accesscontrol.go`** declares all HTTP server fixed roles and their grants to org roles via `declareFixedRoles()`.\n\n## 4. Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance\n\n**`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go`** is the central file for permission inheritance:\n- Defines all action and scope constants (`ActionFoldersRead`, `ActionDashboardsWrite`, `ScopeFoldersPrefix`, etc.)\n- `GetInheritedScopes(ctx, orgID, folderUID, folderSvc)`: walks up the folder tree via `folderSvc.GetParents()` and returns parent folder UID scopes—this is how nested folder permission inheritance works\n- `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver` and `NewFolderIDScopeResolver`: convert folder scopes into UID-based scopes including parent folder scopes\n- `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver` and `NewDashboardIDScopeResolver`: resolve a dashboard scope to include the dashboard's own scope, its parent folder scope, and all ancestor folder scopes\n- `resolveDashboardScope`: calls `GetInheritedScopes` for a dashboard's folder and assembles all applicable scopes\n\n**`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go`** and **`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard_filter_no_subquery.go`** implement SQL-level permission filtering for dashboards and folders, using recursive CTEs to traverse the folder hierarchy and apply inherited permissions.\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go`** implements `IsInherited()` to detect when a managed permission comes from a parent resource scope rather than a direct assignment.\n\n**`pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go`** implements direct-DB RBAC authorization including `checkPermission` and `checkInheritedPermissions` which traverse the folder tree for permission resolution.\n\n**`pkg/services/authz/zanzana/translations.go`** maps Grafana's basic role names to OpenFGA relation names (`basic_viewer`, `basic_editor`, `basic_admin`, etc.) for the Zanzana authorization backend.\n\n**`pkg/services/user/identity.go`** and **`pkg/services/authn/identity.go`** implement `HasRole(role)` which uses `OrgRole.Includes(role)` to check if a user's role encompasses the requested role—enabling the hierarchical check where Admin passes a Viewer check."
}

49: type UIDLookup = func(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, id int64) (string, error)

51: // NewFolderIDScopeResolver provides an ScopeAttributeResolver that is able to convert a scope prefixed with "folders:id:" into an uid based scope.
52: func NewFolderIDScopeResolver(lookup UIDLookup, folderSvc folder.Service) (string, ac.ScopeAttributeResolver) {
53: 	prefix := ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScope("")
54: 	return prefix, ac.ScopeAttributeResolverFunc(func(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, scope string) ([]string, error) {
55: 		ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dashboards.NewFolderIDScopeResolver")
56: 		span.End()
57: 
58: 		if !strings.HasPrefix(scope, prefix) {
59: 			return nil, ac.ErrInvalidScope
60: 		}
61: 
62: 		id, err := ac.ParseScopeID(scope)
63: 		if err != nil {
64: 			return nil, err
65: 		}
66: 
67: 		if id == 0 {
68: 			return []string{ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(ac.GeneralFolderUID)}, nil
69: 		}
70: 
71: 		return identity.WithServiceIdentityFn(ctx, orgID, func(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error) {
72: 			uid, err := lookup(ctx, orgID, id)
73: 			if err != nil {
74: 				return nil, err
75: 			}
76: 
77: 			result, err := GetInheritedScopes(ctx, orgID, uid, folderSvc)
78: 			if err != nil {
79: 				return nil, err
80: 			}
81: 
82: 			return append([]string{ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(uid)}, result...), nil
83: 		})
84: 	})
85: }

106: 			if err != nil {

108: 			}

110: 		})

170: func resolveDashboardScope(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, dashboard *Dashboard, folderSvc folder.Service) ([]string, error) {
171: 	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dashboards.resolveDashboardScope")
172: 	span.End()
173: 
174: 	var folderUID string
175: 	if dashboard.FolderUID == "" {
176: 		folderUID = ac.GeneralFolderUID
177: 	} else {
178: 		folderUID = dashboard.FolderUID
179: 	}
180: 
181: 	result, err := GetInheritedScopes(ctx, orgID, folderUID, folderSvc)
182: 	if err != nil {
183: 		return nil, err
184: 	}
185: 
186: 	result = append([]string{
187: 		ScopeDashboardsProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(dashboard.UID),
188: 		ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(folderUID),
189: 	},
190: 		result...,
191: 	)
192: 
193: 	return result, nil
194: }
195: 
196: func GetInheritedScopes(ctx context.Context, orgID int64, folderUID string, folderSvc folder.Service) ([]string, error) {
197: 	ctx, span := tracer.Start(ctx, "dashboards.GetInheritedScopes")
198: 	span.End()
199: 
200: 	if folderUID == ac.GeneralFolderUID {
201: 		return nil, nil
202: 	}
203: 	ancestors, err := folderSvc.GetParents(ctx, folder.GetParentsQuery{
204: 		UID:   folderUID,
205: 		OrgID: orgID,
206: 	})
207: 
208: 	if err != nil {
209: 		if errors.Is(err, folder.ErrFolderNotFound) {
210: 			return nil, err
211: 		}
212: 		return nil, ac.ErrInternal.Errorf("could not retrieve folder parents: %w", err)
213: 	}
214: 
215: 	result := make([]string, 0, len(ancestors))
216: 	for _, ff := range ancestors {
217: 		result = append(result, ScopeFoldersProvider.GetResourceScopeUID(ff.UID))
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	return result, nil
221: }

  "text": "Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) is implemented across several layers:\n\n## 1. Role Type Definition\n\n**`pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go`** is the foundational file. It defines `RoleType` as a string, the four constants (`RoleNone`, `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, `RoleAdmin`), and the `rolePrecedence` map that establishes the hierarchy (None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40). The key `Includes(other RoleType) bool` method uses this precedence map to check if a role encompasses a lower role (e.g., Admin includes Editor and Viewer). `Parents()` and `Children()` methods also traverse the hierarchy.\n\n**`pkg/services/org/model.go`** re-exports `RoleType` and the role constants from the identity package, and defines `OrgUser` (the DB model linking a user to an org with a `RoleType` role).\n\n## 2. Role-to-Permission Mapping\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go`** defines:\n- Constants like `BasicRolePrefix`, `FixedRolePrefix`, `RoleGrafanaAdmin`, `FixedCloudViewerRole/EditorRole/AdminRole`\n- `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()`: builds the basic role DTOs (basic:viewer, basic:editor, basic:admin, basic:none, basic:grafana_admin) keyed by org role string\n- `DeclareFixedRoles(service, cfg)`: declares all OSS fixed roles and their grants to the three org roles\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go`** and **`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go`** declare the `fixed:folders:viewer/editor/admin` and `fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin` roles, mapping them to permission action sets and granting them to the corresponding basic org roles.\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/service.go`** implements the core access control service including `DeclareFixedRoles`, `GetUserPermissions`, `getBasicRolePermissions`, and `getCachedBasicRolesPermissions`—the logic that resolves what permissions a user has based on their basic role (Viewer/Editor/Admin).\n\n**`pkg/services/accesscontrol/database/database.go`** provides the store layer: `GetUsersBasicRoles` returns a map of userID→[]string of role names (Admin, Editor, Viewer, Grafana Admin), and `GetBasicRolesPermissions` fetches all permissions assigned to the user's basic roles via the `builtin_role` table.\n\n## 3.   "text": "Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin) is implemented across several layers:\n\n## 1. Role Type Definition\n\n**pkg/apimachinery/identity/role_type.go** is the foundational file. It defines `RoleType` as a string, the four constants (RoleNone, RoleViewer, RoleEditor, RoleAdmin), and the `rolePrecedence` map that establishes the hierarchy (None=10, Viewer=20, Editor=30, Admin=40). The key `Includes(other RoleType) bool` method uses this precedence map to check if a role encompasses a lower role (e.g., Admin includes Editor and Viewer). `Parents()` and `Children()` methods also traverse the hierarchy.\n\n**pkg/services/org/model.go** re-exports `RoleType` and the role constants from the identity package, and defines `OrgUser` (the DB model linking a user to an org with a RoleType role).\n\n## 2. Role-to-Permission Mapping\n\n**pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go** defines:\n- Constants like BasicRolePrefix, FixedRolePrefix, RoleGrafanaAdmin, FixedCloudViewerRole/EditorRole/AdminRole\n- `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()`: builds the basic role DTOs (basic:viewer, basic:editor, basic:admin, basic:none, basic:grafana_admin) keyed by org role string\n- `DeclareFixedRoles(service, cfg)`: declares all OSS fixed roles and their grants to the three org roles\n\n**pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go** and **pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go** declare the fixed:folders:viewer/editor/admin and fixed:dashboards:viewer/editor/admin roles, mapping them to permission action sets and granting them to the corresponding basic org roles.\n\n**pkg/services/accesscontrol/acimpl/service.go** implements the core access control service including DeclareFixedRoles, GetUserPermissions, getBasicRolePermissions, and getCachedBasicRolesPermissions - the logic that resolves what permissions a user has based on their basic role (Viewer/Editor/Admin).\n\n**pkg/services/accesscontrol/database/database.go** provides the store layer: GetUsersBasicRoles returns a map of userID to role names (Admin, Editor, Viewer, Grafana Admin), and GetBasicRolesPermissions fetches all permissions assigned to the user's basic roles via the builtin_role table.\n\n## 3. Permission Guard Middleware\n\n**pkg/middleware/middleware.go** defines the pre-built middleware guards:\n- ReqGrafanaAdmin - requires Grafana server admin\n- ReqEditorRole = RoleAuth(org.RoleEditor, org.RoleAdmin) - allows Editor or Admin org role\n- ReqOrgAdmin = RoleAuth(org.RoleAdmin) - allows only Admin org role\n\n**pkg/middleware/auth.go** implements:\n- `RoleAuth(roles ...org.RoleType) web.Handler`: checks if c.OrgRole exactly matches any of the provided roles; denies if not\n- `Auth(options *AuthOptions) web.Handler`: checks sign-in status and Grafana admin flag\n- CanAdminPlugins, RoleAppPluginAuth: additional guards using RBAC evaluators\n\n**pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go** implements the RBAC-based middleware:\n- `Middleware(ac AccessControl) func(Evaluator) web.Handler`: wraps an RBAC Evaluator into a web handler that calls ac.Evaluate() against the signed-in user\n- `authorize(c, ac, user, evaluator)`: injects URL params into scope templates and evaluates access\n- `AuthorizeInOrgMiddleware`: resolves user identity in a target org before evaluating permissions\n\n**pkg/services/apiserver/auth/authorizer/role.go** implements the Kubernetes API server authorizer using org roles: Admin gets full access, Editor gets get/list/watch/create/update/patch/delete/put/post, and Viewer/None get only get/list/watch.\n\n**pkg/api/accesscontrol.go** declares all HTTP server fixed roles and their grants to org roles via declareFixedRoles().\n\n## 4. Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance\n\n**pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go** is the central file for permission inheritance:\n- Defines all action and scope constants (ActionFoldersRead, ActionDashboardsWrite, ScopeFoldersPrefix, etc.)\n- `GetInheritedScopes(ctx, orgID, folderUID, folderSvc)`: walks up the folder tree via folderSvc.GetParents() and returns parent folder UID scopes - this is how nested folder permission inheritance works\n- NewFolderUIDScopeResolver and NewFolderIDScopeResolver: convert folder scopes into UID-based scopes including parent folder scopes\n- NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver and NewDashboardIDScopeResolver: resolve a dashboard scope to include the dashboard's own scope, its parent folder scope, and all ancestor folder scopes\n- `resolveDashboardScope`: calls GetInheritedScopes for a dashboard's folder and assembles all applicable scopes\n\n**pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go** and **pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard_filter_no_subquery.go** implement SQL-level permission filtering for dashboards and folders, using recursive CTEs to traverse the folder hierarchy and apply inherited permissions.\n\n**pkg/services/accesscontrol/resourcepermissions/store.go** implements `IsInherited()` to detect when a managed permission comes from a parent resource scope rather than a direct assignment.\n\n**pkg/services/authz/rbac/service.go** implements direct-DB RBAC authorization including checkPermission and checkInheritedPermissions which traverse the folder tree for permission resolution.\n\n**pkg/services/authz/zanzana/translations.go** maps Grafana's basic role names to OpenFGA relation names (basic_viewer, basic_editor, basic_admin, etc.) for the Zanzana authorization backend.\n\n**pkg/services/user/identity.go** and **pkg/services/authn/identity.go** implement `HasRole(role)` which uses `OrgRole.Includes(role)` to check if a user's role encompasses the requested role - enabling the hierarchical check where Admin passes a Viewer check."
